failureReason: NVIDIA kernel module not loaded and CUDA Toolkit / nvcc not installed on this box as of 2026-05-11. Driver packages (nvidia-driver-580-open 580.126.09) are installed; the operator is bringing up the kernel module + toolkit. Re-run cuda_runner.sh once `nvidia-smi -L` and `nvcc --version` both succeed; this file will be replaced by the real evidence JSON.

toolchain.driverPackages: nvidia-driver-580-open 580.126.09 (libnvidia-compute-580 etc. installed; nvidia.ko not loaded)

requirements.hardware: NVIDIA GPU with nvidia-smi -L showing a device
requirements.toolchain: CUDA Toolkit >= 12.8 for sm_120 device code (older nvcc works via compute_90 PTX JIT)
requirements.fixtureParity: make cuda-verify — turbo3, turbo4, turbo3_tcq, qjl, polar, polar_qjl (and fused_attn_qjl_tbq if landed) all 8/8
requirements.graphSmoke: runtime_graph_smoke.sh with ELIZA_DFLASH_SMOKE_MODEL — llama-cli --cache-type-k for every Turbo/QJL/Polar alias, CUDA backend evidence in the log
requirements.evidence: this file rewritten with status:pass, passRecordable:true, gpuInfo, toolchainInfo, modelSha256

harness.note: cuda_verify.cu ports the C reference (reference/turbo_kernels.c + verify/qjl_polar_ref.c) into CUDA __device__ kernels operating on the on-disk fixture byte layouts — same approach as metal_verify / vulkan_verify. No build of the eliza-llama-cpp fork is required for `make cuda-verify`; the fork build is only needed for the model-backed graph smoke.
